name: Mahatala
description: Mahatala, also known as Rasatala, is one of the seven lower realms or Patalas in Hindu cosmology, situated below the earth. It is considered a region of great beauty and opulence, inhabited by beings of immense power and wealth, including Nagas (serpent deities), Daityas (demons), and other powerful entities. Unlike other lower realms associated with suffering, Mahatala is often depicted as a place of enjoyment and luxury, though detached from the higher spiritual planes. It is said to be richer and more splendid than even the heavens, filled with precious gems and adorned with magnificent palaces. While not necessarily a place of torment, its inhabitants are driven by worldly desires and material pursuits, lacking the spiritual insight of those in the higher realms. The realm is ruled by the Nagas, who are protectors of immense treasures and possess supernatural abilities. The presence of Daityas, though, indicates a potential for conflict and imbalance, even within this seemingly idyllic realm. Mahatala serves as a reminder of the allure and potential pitfalls of material abundance without spiritual grounding. It is described in various Puranas, including the Bhagavata Purana and the Vishnu Purana, each offering slightly different perspectives on its nature and inhabitants. The journey through the Patalas, including Mahatala, represents a descent into the layers of consciousness, where material desires and attachments can either bind or be transcended.
subtitle: A luxurious lower realm of Nagas.

keyMyths.title: Matsya Avatar and the Flood
keyMyths.description: During the great flood, Vishnu in his Matsya (fish) avatar protected Manu and the seeds of creation, guiding them through the turbulent waters that reached even the lower realms, including Mahatala. This event highlights the interconnectedness of all realms and Vishnu's role as preserver.

keyMyths.title: Ananta Shesha's Support
keyMyths.description: The serpent deity Ananta Shesha, upon whom Vishnu rests, supports all the worlds, including Mahatala. He is a symbol of infinite potential and the foundation upon which creation rests, connecting the highest and lowest realms.

sources.author: Bibek Debroy
